finding.contract_claim: Parameter `error` is declared in the signature of log(message, error=False) (src/app/launcher.py line 1) as an accepted input that callers may pass.
finding.observed_behavior: The attributed call site, log("ok") in src/app/cli.py, does not pass `error`, so the parameter appears to always take its default False. `log` is a common name with high fan-out: src/noise.py defines an unrelated log(message) that is called locally.
finding.triage_reasoning:
Reality: The reference sweep covered 3 files and found exactly 1 match for `error` — inside the f-string `f"{message}:{error}"` in the function body itself (an intra-function use, not a caller supplying a value). The attributed call site `log("ok")` in cli.py does not pass `error`. The parameter always takes its default `False`.

Significance: The parameter is functionally dead; every call returns `":False"` appended to the message. Removing it would clean up the API and the misleading return value.

Actionability: Remove the `error` parameter from `log(message, error=False)` and update the return to `return f"{message}"` (or whatever the desired format without the flag is). The mitigating note about `src/noise.py`'s unrelated `log(message)` does not affect this conclusion — the swept scope covers the project, not the noise file's callers.
finding.suggested_fix: Remove the `error` parameter from `log` in `src/app/launcher.py`. Update the return expression to drop `:{error}`. Confirm the single call site `log("ok")` in `src/app/cli.py` requires no changes.
